content:
1 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Most algorithm practice is a pile of random problems. You get reps, but you never build a mental map of the techniques. So I built AnotherCompiler, a place to learn algorithms and data structures through an ordered syllabus instead of a shuffle.
Every problem is grouped by pattern: two pointers, sliding window, prefix sums, hashing, binary search, dynamic programming, greedy, and more. Each section runs from the easy on-ramp to the harder variations, so the technique actually sticks. Difficulty climbs with it. I am still tweaking the exact order, but the recommended path is simple: solve all the easy problems, then all the medium, then all the hard.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

AnotherCompilerYou write and run your solution right in the browser, and on your phone too, in Python, TypeScript, Kotlin, or Java.
It is still growing, and the ordering and problem set keep evolving.
Give it a try: https://anothercompiler.web.app
